closure_boundary: This source document is a post-commit review plan kept inside the repository. Final findings, exact-file review completion, task dispositions, and the release decision are produced outside the candidate after its signed commit exists; this plan does not establish completion.

accepted_cases: Accept T000 “Freeze exact audit inputs and produce a signed immutable input manifest” only after every non-excluded source precondition, procedure, mandatory counterfactual, required-evidence item, completion rule, and exact lens question is addressed with evidence bound to the signed candidate commit and tree.
Accept T000 adaptations only when each excluded source item retains its exact claim-removal link and residual risk; a plan, assignment, path, or command name is not a result.
failed_attempt_record_requirements: T000: retain every failed command, test, counterexample, reproduction, and review attempt with exact inputs, outputs, exit status, and artifact digest.
T000: do not delete, pool away, relabel, or convert a negative, missing, non-estimable, timed-out, or capacity-failed result into completion evidence.
tasks.focus: commit, tree, submodules, tags, toolchains, packages, models/data, papers, downstream heads

requirement_exclusions.reason: The 0.9 source release retains author-operated review and reproducibility only; organizational or clean-room independence is not claimed.
No external reviewer record is represented as available for 0.9.

residual_risks: T000: CLM-012 remains NOT_CLAIMED — The retained review is author-operated and machine-assisted. Review packets and a blank external-comment interface are provided, but no independent person is represented as having reviewed or approved the candidate.
T000: CLM-016 remains NOT_CLAIMED — The isolated-worktree qualifier is author-operated. It records exact inputs and deterministic artifacts but is not organizationally or procedurally independent.
T000: future changes to commit, tree, submodules, tags, toolchains, packages, models/data, papers, downstream heads can invalidate this exact-candidate closure and require a new review.

review_questions.L01: Can a reader infer more than the exact evidence tier supports? Separate implemented, verified, validated, deployment-qualified, field-validated, and not-claimed.
review_questions.L02: Are inputs, state, transitions, outputs, invariants, units, and failure states explicit rather than implied by names?
review_questions.L03: Are definitions, assumptions, estimands, finite-sample behavior, multiplicity, uncertainty, calibration, and failure regions correct and explicit?
review_questions.L04: Can invalid combinations be constructed through safe APIs, deserialization, FFI, feature flags, defaults, or migration?
review_questions.L05: Are clock domains, freshness, deadlines, sessions, epochs, generations, sequence numbers, duplicates, restart, and rollover unambiguous?
review_questions.L06: Can every result, command, model, dataset, scene, schema, configuration, build, and evidence artifact be traced immutably?
review_questions.L07: Are canonical bytes, domain separation, algorithms, identities, rotation, revocation, downgrade resistance, and failure semantics complete?
review_questions.L08: Who may observe, advise, intend, authorize, publish, apply, and confirm? Can any fallback or optional component widen authority?
review_questions.L09: What happens with duplicates, unknown fields, unsafe integers, NaN/Inf, Unicode ambiguity, malformed nesting, path tricks, and contradictory metadata?
review_questions.L10: Are bytes, allocations, dimensions, states, queues, retries, threads, log volume, GPU/CPU work, and serialization bounded before expensive work?
review_questions.L11: Are initialization, callback ownership, races, cancellation, selected events, cleanup, crash consistency, and shutdown fully specified?
review_questions.L12: Can independent clean machines reproduce code generation, builds, fixtures, simulations, proofs, benchmarks, evidence, and archives?
review_questions.L13: Is the stable surface minimal and are panic, ownership, allocation, thread safety, features, MSRV/runtime, and compatibility explicit?
review_questions.L14: Is there one normative semantic source and do all languages/encodings accept and reject the same corpus?
review_questions.L15: Can invalid profiles start? Are paths, secrets, permissions, ACLs, certificates, environments, startup order, and rollback safe?
review_questions.L16: Can disabled, unavailable, stale, incompatible, insufficient, anomalous, denied, and internal-fault states be distinguished without leaking secrets?
review_questions.L17: Does each claim have the right proof: theorem, model, property, fuzz, mutation, clean-room, real-router, statistical, or physical evidence?
review_questions.L18: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Prisoma, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, ROS, and external authority relationships explicit and acyclic?
review_questions.L19: Are naming, docs, examples, runbooks, review roles, incident response, support, deprecation, and withdrawal usable under stress?
review_questions.L20: How would a malicious peer, rushed operator, odd-but-simple dataset, future maintainer, or AI-generated patch falsify the intended claim?

source_requirements.completion_rule: No prose-only closure. Every acceptance condition and evidence item must pass on the exact candidate; otherwise mark NOT_CLAIMED or NO_GO.
mandatory_counterfactuals: valid syntax with contradictory semantics
authenticated but unauthorized producer
stale yet correctly signed data
correct version string with wrong contract/algorithm digest
clean synthetic result under nonrepresentative distribution
timeout or capacity exhaustion followed by convenience fallback
feature unification activating a privileged/experimental path
partial migration accepted through default values
crash between decision/output and durable evidence
simple but odd input unlikely to resemble training examples
preconditions: HEAD equals 94e2f8cc01f352d2bf899b7f656997f143a2588f or an approved updated audit cut exists.
Every in-scope tracked/generated file is present in FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv and assigned to a named reviewer.
The current behavior, public claim tier, assumptions, affected consumers, and rollback are recorded before editing.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line, including tests, examples, generated output, workflow, documentation, and relevant Git history; record blob and SHA-256 identities.
Write a current-behavior table covering inputs, validation, state, transitions, outputs, errors, resources, time, identity, persistence, concurrency, and public claims.
Enumerate all plausible semantic interpretations and construct at least one counterexample or misuse for each rejected interpretation.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable IDs and exact accepted/rejected cases; obtain lead approval before implementation.
Implement the smallest coherent change and delete, quarantine, or version any contradictory legacy/default/fallback path.
Add positive, malformed, empty, min/max, non-finite, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future, capacity, cancellation, restart, and migration tests as applicable.
Add property/metamorphic, differential/oracle, fuzz, mutation-sensitive, concurrency, resource, and integration tests appropriate to the claim tier.
Generate evidence from a clean checkout with exact tools, commands, configs, seeds, data/models, platform/hardware, raw outputs, exit status, and checksums.
Update schemas, API snapshots, migration, examples, README/docs, claim/evidence ledger, support matrix, and exact downstream pins.
Have an independent reviewer reproduce the decisive evidence and review every changed line plus relevant unchanged context.
Run the subsystem gate, complete wave gate, full locked CI, and every affected downstream conformance job.
Close only after all twenty lenses are resolved with evidence; otherwise remove/narrow the claim and leave the task OPEN.
required_evidence: positive and negative vectors or fixtures
coverage, fuzz/mutation or model results where applicable

residual_risks: T003: CLM-012 remains NOT_CLAIMED — The retained review is author-operated and machine-assisted. Review packets and a blank external-comment interface are provided, but no independent person is represented as having reviewed or approved the candidate.
T003: CLM-016 remains NOT_CLAIMED — The isolated-worktree qualifier is author-operated. It records exact inputs and deterministic artifacts but is not organizationally or procedurally independent.
T003: future changes to truthful scope can invalidate this exact-candidate closure and require a new review.

review_questions.L01: Can a reader infer more than the exact evidence tier supports? Separate implemented, verified, validated, deployment-qualified, field-validated, and not-claimed.
review_questions.L02: Are inputs, state, transitions, outputs, invariants, units, and failure states explicit rather than implied by names?
review_questions.L03: Are definitions, assumptions, estimands, finite-sample behavior, multiplicity, uncertainty, calibration, and failure regions correct and explicit?
review_questions.L04: Can invalid combinations be constructed through safe APIs, deserialization, FFI, feature flags, defaults, or migration?
review_questions.L05: Are clock domains, freshness, deadlines, sessions, epochs, generations, sequence numbers, duplicates, restart, and rollover unambiguous?
review_questions.L06: Can every result, command, model, dataset, scene, schema, configuration, build, and evidence artifact be traced immutably?
review_questions.L07: Are canonical bytes, domain separation, algorithms, identities, rotation, revocation, downgrade resistance, and failure semantics complete?
review_questions.L08: Who may observe, advise, intend, authorize, publish, apply, and confirm? Can any fallback or optional component widen authority?
review_questions.L09: What happens with duplicates, unknown fields, unsafe integers, NaN/Inf, Unicode ambiguity, malformed nesting, path tricks, and contradictory metadata?
review_questions.L10: Are bytes, allocations, dimensions, states, queues, retries, threads, log volume, GPU/CPU work, and serialization bounded before expensive work?
review_questions.L11: Are initialization, callback ownership, races, cancellation, selected events, cleanup, crash consistency, and shutdown fully specified?
review_questions.L12: Can independent clean machines reproduce code generation, builds, fixtures, simulations, proofs, benchmarks, evidence, and archives?
review_questions.L13: Is the stable surface minimal and are panic, ownership, allocation, thread safety, features, MSRV/runtime, and compatibility explicit?
review_questions.L14: Is there one normative semantic source and do all languages/encodings accept and reject the same corpus?
review_questions.L15: Can invalid profiles start? Are paths, secrets, permissions, ACLs, certificates, environments, startup order, and rollback safe?
review_questions.L16: Can disabled, unavailable, stale, incompatible, insufficient, anomalous, denied, and internal-fault states be distinguished without leaking secrets?
review_questions.L17: Does each claim have the right proof: theorem, model, property, fuzz, mutation, clean-room, real-router, statistical, or physical evidence?
review_questions.L18: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Prisoma, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, ROS, and external authority relationships explicit and acyclic?
review_questions.L19: Are naming, docs, examples, runbooks, review roles, incident response, support, deprecation, and withdrawal usable under stress?
review_questions.L20: How would a malicious peer, rushed operator, odd-but-simple dataset, future maintainer, or AI-generated patch falsify the intended claim?

source_requirements.completion_rule: No prose-only closure. Every acceptance condition and evidence item must pass on the exact candidate; otherwise mark NOT_CLAIMED or NO_GO.
mandatory_counterfactuals: valid syntax with contradictory semantics
authenticated but unauthorized producer
stale yet correctly signed data
correct version string with wrong contract/algorithm digest
clean synthetic result under nonrepresentative distribution
timeout or capacity exhaustion followed by convenience fallback
feature unification activating a privileged/experimental path
partial migration accepted through default values
crash between decision/output and durable evidence
simple but odd input unlikely to resemble training examples
preconditions: HEAD equals 94e2f8cc01f352d2bf899b7f656997f143a2588f or an approved updated audit cut exists.
Every in-scope tracked/generated file is present in FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv and assigned to a named reviewer.
The current behavior, public claim tier, assumptions, affected consumers, and rollback are recorded before editing.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line, including tests, examples, generated output, workflow, documentation, and relevant Git history; record blob and SHA-256 identities.
Write a current-behavior table covering inputs, validation, state, transitions, outputs, errors, resources, time, identity, persistence, concurrency, and public claims.
Enumerate all plausible semantic interpretations and construct at least one counterexample or misuse for each rejected interpretation.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable IDs and exact accepted/rejected cases; obtain lead approval before implementation.
Implement the smallest coherent change and delete, quarantine, or version any contradictory legacy/default/fallback path.
Add positive, malformed, empty, min/max, non-finite, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future, capacity, cancellation, restart, and migration tests as applicable.
Add property/metamorphic, differential/oracle, fuzz, mutation-sensitive, concurrency, resource, and integration tests appropriate to the claim tier.
Generate evidence from a clean checkout with exact tools, commands, configs, seeds, data/models, platform/hardware, raw outputs, exit status, and checksums.
Update schemas, API snapshots, migration, examples, README/docs, claim/evidence ledger, support matrix, and exact downstream pins.
Have an independent reviewer reproduce the decisive evidence and review every changed line plus relevant unchanged context.
Run the subsystem gate, complete wave gate, full locked CI, and every affected downstream conformance job.
Close only after all twenty lenses are resolved with evidence; otherwise remove/narrow the claim and leave the task OPEN.
required_evidence: positive and negative vectors or fixtures
coverage, fuzz/mutation or model results where applicable

tasks.source_scope: exports, CLI, IPC, schemas, docs, packages, GitHub metadata
tasks.title: Create public surface inventory and claim-tier ledger

accepted_cases: Accept T004 “Create threat, misuse, failure, and quirky-case register” only after every non-excluded source precondition, procedure, mandatory counterfactual, required-evidence item, completion rule, and exact lens question is addressed with evidence bound to the signed candidate commit and tree.
Accept T004 adaptations only when each excluded source item retains its exact claim-removal link and residual risk; a plan, assignment, path, or command name is not a result.
failed_attempt_record_requirements: T004: retain every failed command, test, counterexample, reproduction, and review attempt with exact inputs, outputs, exit status, and artifact digest.
T004: do not delete, pool away, relabel, or convert a negative, missing, non-estimable, timed-out, or capacity-failed result into completion evidence.
tasks.focus: adversarial model

authenticated but unauthorized producer
stale yet correctly signed data
correct version string with wrong contract/algorithm digest
clean synthetic result under nonrepresentative distribution
timeout or capacity exhaustion followed by convenience fallback
feature unification activating a privileged/experimental path
partial migration accepted through default values
crash between decision/output and durable evidence
simple but odd input unlikely to resemble training examples
preconditions: HEAD equals 94e2f8cc01f352d2bf899b7f656997f143a2588f or an approved updated audit cut exists.
Every in-scope tracked/generated file is present in FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv and assigned to a named reviewer.
The current behavior, public claim tier, assumptions, affected consumers, and rollback are recorded before editing.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line, including tests, examples, generated output, workflow, documentation, and relevant Git history; record blob and SHA-256 identities.
Write a current-behavior table covering inputs, validation, state, transitions, outputs, errors, resources, time, identity, persistence, concurrency, and public claims.
Enumerate all plausible semantic interpretations and construct at least one counterexample or misuse for each rejected interpretation.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable IDs and exact accepted/rejected cases; obtain lead approval before implementation.
Implement the smallest coherent change and delete, quarantine, or version any contradictory legacy/default/fallback path.
Add positive, malformed, empty, min/max, non-finite, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future, capacity, cancellation, restart, and migration tests as applicable.
Add property/metamorphic, differential/oracle, fuzz, mutation-sensitive, concurrency, resource, and integration tests appropriate to the claim tier.
Generate evidence from a clean checkout with exact tools, commands, configs, seeds, data/models, platform/hardware, raw outputs, exit status, and checksums.
Update schemas, API snapshots, migration, examples, README/docs, claim/evidence ledger, support matrix, and exact downstream pins.
Have an independent reviewer reproduce the decisive evidence and review every changed line plus relevant unchanged context.
Run the subsystem gate, complete wave gate, full locked CI, and every affected downstream conformance job.
Close only after all twenty lenses are resolved with evidence; otherwise remove/narrow the claim and leave the task OPEN.
required_evidence: positive and negative vectors or fixtures
coverage, fuzz/mutation or model results where applicable

tasks.title: Inventory TODO/FIXME/HACK/experimental/unimplemented/dormant and permissive fallback paths

accepted_cases: Accept T007 “Inventory dependencies, features, code generators, licenses, assets, datasets, and supply-chain trust” only after every non-excluded source precondition, procedure, mandatory counterfactual, required-evidence item, completion rule, and exact lens question is addressed with evidence bound to the signed candidate commit and tree.
Accept T007 adaptations only when each excluded source item retains its exact claim-removal link and residual risk; a plan, assignment, path, or command name is not a result.
failed_attempt_record_requirements: T007: retain every failed command, test, counterexample, reproduction, and review attempt with exact inputs, outputs, exit status, and artifact digest.
T007: do not delete, pool away, relabel, or convert a negative, missing, non-estimable, timed-out, or capacity-failed result into completion evidence.

requirement_exclusions.reason: The 0.9 source release retains author-operated review and reproducibility only; organizational or clean-room independence is not claimed.
No external reviewer record is represented as available for 0.9.

residual_risks: T007: CLM-012 remains NOT_CLAIMED — The retained review is author-operated and machine-assisted. Review packets and a blank external-comment interface are provided, but no independent person is represented as having reviewed or approved the candidate.
T007: CLM-016 remains NOT_CLAIMED — The isolated-worktree qualifier is author-operated. It records exact inputs and deterministic artifacts but is not organizationally or procedurally independent.
T007: future changes to dependency governance can invalidate this exact-candidate closure and require a new review.

review_questions.L01: Can a reader infer more than the exact evidence tier supports? Separate implemented, verified, validated, deployment-qualified, field-validated, and not-claimed.
review_questions.L02: Are inputs, state, transitions, outputs, invariants, units, and failure states explicit rather than implied by names?
review_questions.L03: Are definitions, assumptions, estimands, finite-sample behavior, multiplicity, uncertainty, calibration, and failure regions correct and explicit?
review_questions.L04: Can invalid combinations be constructed through safe APIs, deserialization, FFI, feature flags, defaults, or migration?
review_questions.L05: Are clock domains, freshness, deadlines, sessions, epochs, generations, sequence numbers, duplicates, restart, and rollover unambiguous?
review_questions.L06: Can every result, command, model, dataset, scene, schema, configuration, build, and evidence artifact be traced immutably?
review_questions.L07: Are canonical bytes, domain separation, algorithms, identities, rotation, revocation, downgrade resistance, and failure semantics complete?
review_questions.L08: Who may observe, advise, intend, authorize, publish, apply, and confirm? Can any fallback or optional component widen authority?
review_questions.L09: What happens with duplicates, unknown fields, unsafe integers, NaN/Inf, Unicode ambiguity, malformed nesting, path tricks, and contradictory metadata?
review_questions.L10: Are bytes, allocations, dimensions, states, queues, retries, threads, log volume, GPU/CPU work, and serialization bounded before expensive work?
review_questions.L11: Are initialization, callback ownership, races, cancellation, selected events, cleanup, crash consistency, and shutdown fully specified?
review_questions.L12: Can independent clean machines reproduce code generation, builds, fixtures, simulations, proofs, benchmarks, evidence, and archives?
review_questions.L13: Is the stable surface minimal and are panic, ownership, allocation, thread safety, features, MSRV/runtime, and compatibility explicit?
review_questions.L14: Is there one normative semantic source and do all languages/encodings accept and reject the same corpus?
review_questions.L15: Can invalid profiles start? Are paths, secrets, permissions, ACLs, certificates, environments, startup order, and rollback safe?
review_questions.L16: Can disabled, unavailable, stale, incompatible, insufficient, anomalous, denied, and internal-fault states be distinguished without leaking secrets?
review_questions.L17: Does each claim have the right proof: theorem, model, property, fuzz, mutation, clean-room, real-router, statistical, or physical evidence?
review_questions.L18: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Prisoma, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, ROS, and external authority relationships explicit and acyclic?
review_questions.L19: Are naming, docs, examples, runbooks, review roles, incident response, support, deprecation, and withdrawal usable under stress?
review_questions.L20: How would a malicious peer, rushed operator, odd-but-simple dataset, future maintainer, or AI-generated patch falsify the intended claim?

No external reviewer record is represented as available for 0.9.

residual_risks: T010: CLM-012 remains NOT_CLAIMED — The retained review is author-operated and machine-assisted. Review packets and a blank external-comment interface are provided, but no independent person is represented as having reviewed or approved the candidate.
T010: CLM-016 remains NOT_CLAIMED — The isolated-worktree qualifier is author-operated. It records exact inputs and deterministic artifacts but is not organizationally or procedurally independent.

review_questions.L01: Can a reader infer more than the exact evidence tier supports? Separate implemented, verified, validated, deployment-qualified, field-validated, and not-claimed.
review_questions.L02: Are inputs, state, transitions, outputs, invariants, units, and failure states explicit rather than implied by names?
review_questions.L03: Are definitions, assumptions, estimands, finite-sample behavior, multiplicity, uncertainty, calibration, and failure regions correct and explicit?
review_questions.L04: Can invalid combinations be constructed through safe APIs, deserialization, FFI, feature flags, defaults, or migration?
review_questions.L05: Are clock domains, freshness, deadlines, sessions, epochs, generations, sequence numbers, duplicates, restart, and rollover unambiguous?
review_questions.L06: Can every result, command, model, dataset, scene, schema, configuration, build, and evidence artifact be traced immutably?
review_questions.L07: Are canonical bytes, domain separation, algorithms, identities, rotation, revocation, downgrade resistance, and failure semantics complete?
review_questions.L08: Who may observe, advise, intend, authorize, publish, apply, and confirm? Can any fallback or optional component widen authority?
review_questions.L09: What happens with duplicates, unknown fields, unsafe integers, NaN/Inf, Unicode ambiguity, malformed nesting, path tricks, and contradictory metadata?
review_questions.L10: Are bytes, allocations, dimensions, states, queues, retries, threads, log volume, GPU/CPU work, and serialization bounded before expensive work?
review_questions.L11: Are initialization, callback ownership, races, cancellation, selected events, cleanup, crash consistency, and shutdown fully specified?
review_questions.L12: Can independent clean machines reproduce code generation, builds, fixtures, simulations, proofs, benchmarks, evidence, and archives?
review_questions.L13: Is the stable surface minimal and are panic, ownership, allocation, thread safety, features, MSRV/runtime, and compatibility explicit?
review_questions.L14: Is there one normative semantic source and do all languages/encodings accept and reject the same corpus?
review_questions.L15: Can invalid profiles start? Are paths, secrets, permissions, ACLs, certificates, environments, startup order, and rollback safe?
review_questions.L16: Can disabled, unavailable, stale, incompatible, insufficient, anomalous, denied, and internal-fault states be distinguished without leaking secrets?
review_questions.L17: Does each claim have the right proof: theorem, model, property, fuzz, mutation, clean-room, real-router, statistical, or physical evidence?
review_questions.L18: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Prisoma, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, ROS, and external authority relationships explicit and acyclic?
review_questions.L19: Are naming, docs, examples, runbooks, review roles, incident response, support, deprecation, and withdrawal usable under stress?
review_questions.L20: How would a malicious peer, rushed operator, odd-but-simple dataset, future maintainer, or AI-generated patch falsify the intended claim?

source_requirements.completion_rule: No prose-only closure. Every acceptance condition and evidence item must pass on the exact candidate; otherwise mark NOT_CLAIMED or NO_GO.
mandatory_counterfactuals: valid syntax with contradictory semantics
authenticated but unauthorized producer
stale yet correctly signed data
correct version string with wrong contract/algorithm digest
clean synthetic result under nonrepresentative distribution
timeout or capacity exhaustion followed by convenience fallback
feature unification activating a privileged/experimental path
partial migration accepted through default values
crash between decision/output and durable evidence
simple but odd input unlikely to resemble training examples
preconditions: HEAD equals 94e2f8cc01f352d2bf899b7f656997f143a2588f or an approved updated audit cut exists.
Every in-scope tracked/generated file is present in FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv and assigned to a named reviewer.
The current behavior, public claim tier, assumptions, affected consumers, and rollback are recorded before editing.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line, including tests, examples, generated output, workflow, documentation, and relevant Git history; record blob and SHA-256 identities.
Write a current-behavior table covering inputs, validation, state, transitions, outputs, errors, resources, time, identity, persistence, concurrency, and public claims.
Enumerate all plausible semantic interpretations and construct at least one counterexample or misuse for each rejected interpretation.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable IDs and exact accepted/rejected cases; obtain lead approval before implementation.
Implement the smallest coherent change and delete, quarantine, or version any contradictory legacy/default/fallback path.
Add positive, malformed, empty, min/max, non-finite, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future, capacity, cancellation, restart, and migration tests as applicable.
Add property/metamorphic, differential/oracle, fuzz, mutation-sensitive, concurrency, resource, and integration tests appropriate to the claim tier.
Generate evidence from a clean checkout with exact tools, commands, configs, seeds, data/models, platform/hardware, raw outputs, exit status, and checksums.
Update schemas, API snapshots, migration, examples, README/docs, claim/evidence ledger, support matrix, and exact downstream pins.
Have an independent reviewer reproduce the decisive evidence and review every changed line plus relevant unchanged context.
Run the subsystem gate, complete wave gate, full locked CI, and every affected downstream conformance job.
Close only after all twenty lenses are resolved with evidence; otherwise remove/narrow the claim and leave the task OPEN.
required_evidence: positive and negative vectors or fixtures
coverage, fuzz/mutation or model results where applicable

review_questions.L01: Can a reader infer more than the exact evidence tier supports? Separate implemented, verified, validated, deployment-qualified, field-validated, and not-claimed.
review_questions.L02: Are inputs, state, transitions, outputs, invariants, units, and failure states explicit rather than implied by names?
review_questions.L03: Are definitions, assumptions, estimands, finite-sample behavior, multiplicity, uncertainty, calibration, and failure regions correct and explicit?
review_questions.L04: Can invalid combinations be constructed through safe APIs, deserialization, FFI, feature flags, defaults, or migration?
review_questions.L05: Are clock domains, freshness, deadlines, sessions, epochs, generations, sequence numbers, duplicates, restart, and rollover unambiguous?
review_questions.L06: Can every result, command, model, dataset, scene, schema, configuration, build, and evidence artifact be traced immutably?
review_questions.L07: Are canonical bytes, domain separation, algorithms, identities, rotation, revocation, downgrade resistance, and failure semantics complete?
review_questions.L08: Who may observe, advise, intend, authorize, publish, apply, and confirm? Can any fallback or optional component widen authority?
review_questions.L09: What happens with duplicates, unknown fields, unsafe integers, NaN/Inf, Unicode ambiguity, malformed nesting, path tricks, and contradictory metadata?
review_questions.L10: Are bytes, allocations, dimensions, states, queues, retries, threads, log volume, GPU/CPU work, and serialization bounded before expensive work?
review_questions.L11: Are initialization, callback ownership, races, cancellation, selected events, cleanup, crash consistency, and shutdown fully specified?
review_questions.L12: Can independent clean machines reproduce code generation, builds, fixtures, simulations, proofs, benchmarks, evidence, and archives?
review_questions.L13: Is the stable surface minimal and are panic, ownership, allocation, thread safety, features, MSRV/runtime, and compatibility explicit?
review_questions.L14: Is there one normative semantic source and do all languages/encodings accept and reject the same corpus?
review_questions.L15: Can invalid profiles start? Are paths, secrets, permissions, ACLs, certificates, environments, startup order, and rollback safe?
review_questions.L16: Can disabled, unavailable, stale, incompatible, insufficient, anomalous, denied, and internal-fault states be distinguished without leaking secrets?
review_questions.L17: Does each claim have the right proof: theorem, model, property, fuzz, mutation, clean-room, real-router, statistical, or physical evidence?
review_questions.L18: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Prisoma, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, ROS, and external authority relationships explicit and acyclic?
review_questions.L19: Are naming, docs, examples, runbooks, review roles, incident response, support, deprecation, and withdrawal usable under stress?
review_questions.L20: How would a malicious peer, rushed operator, odd-but-simple dataset, future maintainer, or AI-generated patch falsify the intended claim?

source_requirements.completion_rule: No prose-only closure. Every acceptance condition and evidence item must pass on the exact candidate; otherwise mark NOT_CLAIMED or NO_GO.
mandatory_counterfactuals: valid syntax with contradictory semantics
authenticated but unauthorized producer
stale yet correctly signed data
correct version string with wrong contract/algorithm digest
clean synthetic result under nonrepresentative distribution
timeout or capacity exhaustion followed by convenience fallback
feature unification activating a privileged/experimental path
partial migration accepted through default values
crash between decision/output and durable evidence
simple but odd input unlikely to resemble training examples
preconditions: HEAD equals 94e2f8cc01f352d2bf899b7f656997f143a2588f or an approved updated audit cut exists.
Every in-scope tracked/generated file is present in FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv and assigned to a named reviewer.
The current behavior, public claim tier, assumptions, affected consumers, and rollback are recorded before editing.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line, including tests, examples, generated output, workflow, documentation, and relevant Git history; record blob and SHA-256 identities.
Write a current-behavior table covering inputs, validation, state, transitions, outputs, errors, resources, time, identity, persistence, concurrency, and public claims.
Enumerate all plausible semantic interpretations and construct at least one counterexample or misuse for each rejected interpretation.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable IDs and exact accepted/rejected cases; obtain lead approval before implementation.
Implement the smallest coherent change and delete, quarantine, or version any contradictory legacy/default/fallback path.
Add positive, malformed, empty, min/max, non-finite, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future, capacity, cancellation, restart, and migration tests as applicable.
Add property/metamorphic, differential/oracle, fuzz, mutation-sensitive, concurrency, resource, and integration tests appropriate to the claim tier.
Generate evidence from a clean checkout with exact tools, commands, configs, seeds, data/models, platform/hardware, raw outputs, exit status, and checksums.
Update schemas, API snapshots, migration, examples, README/docs, claim/evidence ledger, support matrix, and exact downstream pins.
Have an independent reviewer reproduce the decisive evidence and review every changed line plus relevant unchanged context.
Run the subsystem gate, complete wave gate, full locked CI, and every affected downstream conformance job.
Close only after all twenty lenses are resolved with evidence; otherwise remove/narrow the claim and leave the task OPEN.

coverage, fuzz/mutation or model results where applicable

review_questions.L01: Can a reader infer more than the exact evidence tier supports? Separate implemented, verified, validated, deployment-qualified, field-validated, and not-claimed.
review_questions.L02: Are inputs, state, transitions, outputs, invariants, units, and failure states explicit rather than implied by names?
review_questions.L03: Are definitions, assumptions, estimands, finite-sample behavior, multiplicity, uncertainty, calibration, and failure regions correct and explicit?
review_questions.L04: Can invalid combinations be constructed through safe APIs, deserialization, FFI, feature flags, defaults, or migration?
review_questions.L05: Are clock domains, freshness, deadlines, sessions, epochs, generations, sequence numbers, duplicates, restart, and rollover unambiguous?
review_questions.L06: Can every result, command, model, dataset, scene, schema, configuration, build, and evidence artifact be traced immutably?
review_questions.L07: Are canonical bytes, domain separation, algorithms, identities, rotation, revocation, downgrade resistance, and failure semantics complete?
review_questions.L08: Who may observe, advise, intend, authorize, publish, apply, and confirm? Can any fallback or optional component widen authority?
review_questions.L09: What happens with duplicates, unknown fields, unsafe integers, NaN/Inf, Unicode ambiguity, malformed nesting, path tricks, and contradictory metadata?
review_questions.L10: Are bytes, allocations, dimensions, states, queues, retries, threads, log volume, GPU/CPU work, and serialization bounded before expensive work?
review_questions.L11: Are initialization, callback ownership, races, cancellation, selected events, cleanup, crash consistency, and shutdown fully specified?
review_questions.L12: Can independent clean machines reproduce code generation, builds, fixtures, simulations, proofs, benchmarks, evidence, and archives?
review_questions.L13: Is the stable surface minimal and are panic, ownership, allocation, thread safety, features, MSRV/runtime, and compatibility explicit?
review_questions.L14: Is there one normative semantic source and do all languages/encodings accept and reject the same corpus?
review_questions.L15: Can invalid profiles start? Are paths, secrets, permissions, ACLs, certificates, environments, startup order, and rollback safe?
review_questions.L16: Can disabled, unavailable, stale, incompatible, insufficient, anomalous, denied, and internal-fault states be distinguished without leaking secrets?
review_questions.L17: Does each claim have the right proof: theorem, model, property, fuzz, mutation, clean-room, real-router, statistical, or physical evidence?
review_questions.L18: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Prisoma, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, ROS, and external authority relationships explicit and acyclic?
review_questions.L19: Are naming, docs, examples, runbooks, review roles, incident response, support, deprecation, and withdrawal usable under stress?
review_questions.L20: How would a malicious peer, rushed operator, odd-but-simple dataset, future maintainer, or AI-generated patch falsify the intended claim?

review_questions.L01: Can a reader infer more than the exact evidence tier supports? Separate implemented, verified, validated, deployment-qualified, field-validated, and not-claimed.
review_questions.L02: Are inputs, state, transitions, outputs, invariants, units, and failure states explicit rather than implied by names?
review_questions.L03: Are definitions, assumptions, estimands, finite-sample behavior, multiplicity, uncertainty, calibration, and failure regions correct and explicit?
review_questions.L04: Can invalid combinations be constructed through safe APIs, deserialization, FFI, feature flags, defaults, or migration?
review_questions.L05: Are clock domains, freshness, deadlines, sessions, epochs, generations, sequence numbers, duplicates, restart, and rollover unambiguous?
review_questions.L06: Can every result, command, model, dataset, scene, schema, configuration, build, and evidence artifact be traced immutably?
review_questions.L07: Are canonical bytes, domain separation, algorithms, identities, rotation, revocation, downgrade resistance, and failure semantics complete?
review_questions.L08: Who may observe, advise, intend, authorize, publish, apply, and confirm? Can any fallback or optional component widen authority?
review_questions.L09: What happens with duplicates, unknown fields, unsafe integers, NaN/Inf, Unicode ambiguity, malformed nesting, path tricks, and contradictory metadata?
review_questions.L10: Are bytes, allocations, dimensions, states, queues, retries, threads, log volume, GPU/CPU work, and serialization bounded before expensive work?
review_questions.L11: Are initialization, callback ownership, races, cancellation, selected events, cleanup, crash consistency, and shutdown fully specified?
review_questions.L12: Can independent clean machines reproduce code generation, builds, fixtures, simulations, proofs, benchmarks, evidence, and archives?
review_questions.L13: Is the stable surface minimal and are panic, ownership, allocation, thread safety, features, MSRV/runtime, and compatibility explicit?
review_questions.L14: Is there one normative semantic source and do all languages/encodings accept and reject the same corpus?
review_questions.L15: Can invalid profiles start? Are paths, secrets, permissions, ACLs, certificates, environments, startup order, and rollback safe?
review_questions.L16: Can disabled, unavailable, stale, incompatible, insufficient, anomalous, denied, and internal-fault states be distinguished without leaking secrets?
review_questions.L17: Does each claim have the right proof: theorem, model, property, fuzz, mutation, clean-room, real-router, statistical, or physical evidence?
review_questions.L18: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Prisoma, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, ROS, and external authority relationships explicit and acyclic?
review_questions.L19: Are naming, docs, examples, runbooks, review roles, incident response, support, deprecation, and withdrawal usable under stress?
review_questions.L20: How would a malicious peer, rushed operator, odd-but-simple dataset, future maintainer, or AI-generated patch falsify the intended claim?

preconditions: HEAD equals 94e2f8cc01f352d2bf899b7f656997f143a2588f or an approved updated audit cut exists.
Every in-scope tracked/generated file is present in FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv and assigned to a named reviewer.
The current behavior, public claim tier, assumptions, affected consumers, and rollback are recorded before editing.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line, including tests, examples, generated output, workflow, documentation, and relevant Git history; record blob and SHA-256 identities.
Write a current-behavior table covering inputs, validation, state, transitions, outputs, errors, resources, time, identity, persistence, concurrency, and public claims.
Enumerate all plausible semantic interpretations and construct at least one counterexample or misuse for each rejected interpretation.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable IDs and exact accepted/rejected cases; obtain lead approval before implementation.
Implement the smallest coherent change and delete, quarantine, or version any contradictory legacy/default/fallback path.
Add positive, malformed, empty, min/max, non-finite, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future, capacity, cancellation, restart, and migration tests as applicable.
Add property/metamorphic, differential/oracle, fuzz, mutation-sensitive, concurrency, resource, and integration tests appropriate to the claim tier.
Generate evidence from a clean checkout with exact tools, commands, configs, seeds, data/models, platform/hardware, raw outputs, exit status, and checksums.
Update schemas, API snapshots, migration, examples, README/docs, claim/evidence ledger, support matrix, and exact downstream pins.
Have an independent reviewer reproduce the decisive evidence and review every changed line plus relevant unchanged context.
Run the subsystem gate, complete wave gate, full locked CI, and every affected downstream conformance job.
Close only after all twenty lenses are resolved with evidence; otherwise remove/narrow the claim and leave the task OPEN.

coverage, fuzz/mutation or model results where applicable

review_questions.L03: Are definitions, assumptions, estimands, finite-sample behavior, multiplicity, uncertainty, calibration, and failure regions correct and explicit?
review_questions.L04: Can invalid combinations be constructed through safe APIs, deserialization, FFI, feature flags, defaults, or migration?
review_questions.L05: Are clock domains, freshness, deadlines, sessions, epochs, generations, sequence numbers, duplicates, restart, and rollover unambiguous?
review_questions.L06: Can every result, command, model, dataset, scene, schema, configuration, build, and evidence artifact be traced immutably?
review_questions.L07: Are canonical bytes, domain separation, algorithms, identities, rotation, revocation, downgrade resistance, and failure semantics complete?
review_questions.L08: Who may observe, advise, intend, authorize, publish, apply, and confirm? Can any fallback or optional component widen authority?
review_questions.L09: What happens with duplicates, unknown fields, unsafe integers, NaN/Inf, Unicode ambiguity, malformed nesting, path tricks, and contradictory metadata?
review_questions.L10: Are bytes, allocations, dimensions, states, queues, retries, threads, log volume, GPU/CPU work, and serialization bounded before expensive work?
review_questions.L11: Are initialization, callback ownership, races, cancellation, selected events, cleanup, crash consistency, and shutdown fully specified?
review_questions.L12: Can independent clean machines reproduce code generation, builds, fixtures, simulations, proofs, benchmarks, evidence, and archives?
review_questions.L13: Is the stable surface minimal and are panic, ownership, allocation, thread safety, features, MSRV/runtime, and compatibility explicit?
review_questions.L14: Is there one normative semantic source and do all languages/encodings accept and reject the same corpus?
review_questions.L15: Can invalid profiles start? Are paths, secrets, permissions, ACLs, certificates, environments, startup order, and rollback safe?
review_questions.L16: Can disabled, unavailable, stale, incompatible, insufficient, anomalous, denied, and internal-fault states be distinguished without leaking secrets?
review_questions.L17: Does each claim have the right proof: theorem, model, property, fuzz, mutation, clean-room, real-router, statistical, or physical evidence?
review_questions.L18: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Prisoma, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, ROS, and external authority relationships explicit and acyclic?
review_questions.L19: Are naming, docs, examples, runbooks, review roles, incident response, support, deprecation, and withdrawal usable under stress?
review_questions.L20: How would a malicious peer, rushed operator, odd-but-simple dataset, future maintainer, or AI-generated patch falsify the intended claim?

source_requirements.completion_rule: No prose-only closure. Every acceptance condition and evidence item must pass on the exact candidate; otherwise mark NOT_CLAIMED or NO_GO.
mandatory_counterfactuals: valid syntax with contradictory semantics
authenticated but unauthorized producer
stale yet correctly signed data
correct version string with wrong contract/algorithm digest
clean synthetic result under nonrepresentative distribution
timeout or capacity exhaustion followed by convenience fallback
feature unification activating a privileged/experimental path
partial migration accepted through default values
crash between decision/output and durable evidence
simple but odd input unlikely to resemble training examples
preconditions: HEAD equals 94e2f8cc01f352d2bf899b7f656997f143a2588f or an approved updated audit cut exists.
Every in-scope tracked/generated file is present in FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv and assigned to a named reviewer.
The current behavior, public claim tier, assumptions, affected consumers, and rollback are recorded before editing.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line, including tests, examples, generated output, workflow, documentation, and relevant Git history; record blob and SHA-256 identities.
Write a current-behavior table covering inputs, validation, state, transitions, outputs, errors, resources, time, identity, persistence, concurrency, and public claims.
Enumerate all plausible semantic interpretations and construct at least one counterexample or misuse for each rejected interpretation.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable IDs and exact accepted/rejected cases; obtain lead approval before implementation.
Implement the smallest coherent change and delete, quarantine, or version any contradictory legacy/default/fallback path.
Add positive, malformed, empty, min/max, non-finite, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future, capacity, cancellation, restart, and migration tests as applicable.
Add property/metamorphic, differential/oracle, fuzz, mutation-sensitive, concurrency, resource, and integration tests appropriate to the claim tier.
Generate evidence from a clean checkout with exact tools, commands, configs, seeds, data/models, platform/hardware, raw outputs, exit status, and checksums.
Update schemas, API snapshots, migration, examples, README/docs, claim/evidence ledger, support matrix, and exact downstream pins.
Have an independent reviewer reproduce the decisive evidence and review every changed line plus relevant unchanged context.
Run the subsystem gate, complete wave gate, full locked CI, and every affected downstream conformance job.
Close only after all twenty lenses are resolved with evidence; otherwise remove/narrow the claim and leave the task OPEN.
required_evidence: positive and negative vectors or fixtures
coverage, fuzz/mutation or model results where applicable

authenticated but unauthorized producer
stale yet correctly signed data
correct version string with wrong contract/algorithm digest
clean synthetic result under nonrepresentative distribution
timeout or capacity exhaustion followed by convenience fallback
feature unification activating a privileged/experimental path
partial migration accepted through default values
crash between decision/output and durable evidence
simple but odd input unlikely to resemble training examples

procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line, including tests, examples, generated output, workflow, documentation, and relevant Git history; record blob and SHA-256 identities.
Write a current-behavior table covering inputs, validation, state, transitions, outputs, errors, resources, time, identity, persistence, concurrency, and public claims.
Enumerate all plausible semantic interpretations and construct at least one counterexample or misuse for each rejected interpretation.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable IDs and exact accepted/rejected cases; obtain lead approval before implementation.
Implement the smallest coherent change and delete, quarantine, or version any contradictory legacy/default/fallback path.
Add positive, malformed, empty, min/max, non-finite, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future, capacity, cancellation, restart, and migration tests as applicable.
Add property/metamorphic, differential/oracle, fuzz, mutation-sensitive, concurrency, resource, and integration tests appropriate to the claim tier.
Generate evidence from a clean checkout with exact tools, commands, configs, seeds, data/models, platform/hardware, raw outputs, exit status, and checksums.
Update schemas, API snapshots, migration, examples, README/docs, claim/evidence ledger, support matrix, and exact downstream pins.
Have an independent reviewer reproduce the decisive evidence and review every changed line plus relevant unchanged context.
Run the subsystem gate, complete wave gate, full locked CI, and every affected downstream conformance job.
Close only after all twenty lenses are resolved with evidence; otherwise remove/narrow the claim and leave the task OPEN.

coverage, fuzz/mutation or model results where applicable

review_questions.L01: Can a reader infer more than the exact evidence tier supports? Separate implemented, verified, validated, deployment-qualified, field-validated, and not-claimed.
review_questions.L02: Are inputs, state, transitions, outputs, invariants, units, and failure states explicit rather than implied by names?
review_questions.L03: Are definitions, assumptions, estimands, finite-sample behavior, multiplicity, uncertainty, calibration, and failure regions correct and explicit?
review_questions.L04: Can invalid combinations be constructed through safe APIs, deserialization, FFI, feature flags, defaults, or migration?
review_questions.L05: Are clock domains, freshness, deadlines, sessions, epochs, generations, sequence numbers, duplicates, restart, and rollover unambiguous?
review_questions.L06: Can every result, command, model, dataset, scene, schema, configuration, build, and evidence artifact be traced immutably?
review_questions.L07: Are canonical bytes, domain separation, algorithms, identities, rotation, revocation, downgrade resistance, and failure semantics complete?
review_questions.L08: Who may observe, advise, intend, authorize, publish, apply, and confirm? Can any fallback or optional component widen authority?
review_questions.L09: What happens with duplicates, unknown fields, unsafe integers, NaN/Inf, Unicode ambiguity, malformed nesting, path tricks, and contradictory metadata?
review_questions.L10: Are bytes, allocations, dimensions, states, queues, retries, threads, log volume, GPU/CPU work, and serialization bounded before expensive work?
review_questions.L11: Are initialization, callback ownership, races, cancellation, selected events, cleanup, crash consistency, and shutdown fully specified?
review_questions.L12: Can independent clean machines reproduce code generation, builds, fixtures, simulations, proofs, benchmarks, evidence, and archives?
review_questions.L13: Is the stable surface minimal and are panic, ownership, allocation, thread safety, features, MSRV/runtime, and compatibility explicit?
review_questions.L14: Is there one normative semantic source and do all languages/encodings accept and reject the same corpus?
review_questions.L15: Can invalid profiles start? Are paths, secrets, permissions, ACLs, certificates, environments, startup order, and rollback safe?
review_questions.L16: Can disabled, unavailable, stale, incompatible, insufficient, anomalous, denied, and internal-fault states be distinguished without leaking secrets?
review_questions.L17: Does each claim have the right proof: theorem, model, property, fuzz, mutation, clean-room, real-router, statistical, or physical evidence?
review_questions.L18: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Prisoma, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, ROS, and external authority relationships explicit and acyclic?
review_questions.L19: Are naming, docs, examples, runbooks, review roles, incident response, support, deprecation, and withdrawal usable under stress?
review_questions.L20: How would a malicious peer, rushed operator, odd-but-simple dataset, future maintainer, or AI-generated patch falsify the intended claim?

Accept T033 adaptations only when each excluded source item retains its exact claim-removal link and residual risk; a plan, assignment, path, or command name is not a result.
failed_attempt_record_requirements: T033: retain every failed command, test, counterexample, reproduction, and review attempt with exact inputs, outputs, exit status, and artifact digest.
T033: do not delete, pool away, relabel, or convert a negative, missing, non-estimable, timed-out, or capacity-failed result into completion evidence.
tasks.focus: Complete truth table, evidence monotonicity, unavailable states, missingness, severity versus confidence, hysteresis, and no Nominal on incomplete prerequisites.

requirement_exclusions.reason: The 0.9 source release retains author-operated review and reproducibility only; organizational or clean-room independence is not claimed.
No external reviewer record is represented as available for 0.9.

residual_risks: T033: CLM-012 remains NOT_CLAIMED — The retained review is author-operated and machine-assisted. Review packets and a blank external-comment interface are provided, but no independent person is represented as having reviewed or approved the candidate.
T033: CLM-016 remains NOT_CLAIMED — The isolated-worktree qualifier is author-operated. It records exact inputs and deterministic artifacts but is not organizationally or procedurally independent.

T033: future changes to Complete truth table, evidence monotonicity, unavailable states, missingness, severity versus confidence, hysteresis, and no Nominal on incomplete prerequisites. can invalidate this exact-candidate closure and require a new review.

review_questions.L01: Can a reader infer more than the exact evidence tier supports? Separate implemented, verified, validated, deployment-qualified, field-validated, and not-claimed.
review_questions.L02: Are inputs, state, transitions, outputs, invariants, units, and failure states explicit rather than implied by names?
review_questions.L03: Are definitions, assumptions, estimands, finite-sample behavior, multiplicity, uncertainty, calibration, and failure regions correct and explicit?
review_questions.L04: Can invalid combinations be constructed through safe APIs, deserialization, FFI, feature flags, defaults, or migration?
review_questions.L05: Are clock domains, freshness, deadlines, sessions, epochs, generations, sequence numbers, duplicates, restart, and rollover unambiguous?
review_questions.L06: Can every result, command, model, dataset, scene, schema, configuration, build, and evidence artifact be traced immutably?
review_questions.L07: Are canonical bytes, domain separation, algorithms, identities, rotation, revocation, downgrade resistance, and failure semantics complete?
review_questions.L08: Who may observe, advise, intend, authorize, publish, apply, and confirm? Can any fallback or optional component widen authority?
review_questions.L09: What happens with duplicates, unknown fields, unsafe integers, NaN/Inf, Unicode ambiguity, malformed nesting, path tricks, and contradictory metadata?
review_questions.L10: Are bytes, allocations, dimensions, states, queues, retries, threads, log volume, GPU/CPU work, and serialization bounded before expensive work?
review_questions.L11: Are initialization, callback ownership, races, cancellation, selected events, cleanup, crash consistency, and shutdown fully specified?
review_questions.L12: Can independent clean machines reproduce code generation, builds, fixtures, simulations, proofs, benchmarks, evidence, and archives?
review_questions.L13: Is the stable surface minimal and are panic, ownership, allocation, thread safety, features, MSRV/runtime, and compatibility explicit?
review_questions.L14: Is there one normative semantic source and do all languages/encodings accept and reject the same corpus?
review_questions.L15: Can invalid profiles start? Are paths, secrets, permissions, ACLs, certificates, environments, startup order, and rollback safe?
review_questions.L16: Can disabled, unavailable, stale, incompatible, insufficient, anomalous, denied, and internal-fault states be distinguished without leaking secrets?
review_questions.L17: Does each claim have the right proof: theorem, model, property, fuzz, mutation, clean-room, real-router, statistical, or physical evidence?
review_questions.L18: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Prisoma, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, ROS, and external authority relationships explicit and acyclic?
review_questions.L19: Are naming, docs, examples, runbooks, review roles, incident response, support, deprecation, and withdrawal usable under stress?
review_questions.L20: How would a malicious peer, rushed operator, odd-but-simple dataset, future maintainer, or AI-generated patch falsify the intended claim?

source_requirements.completion_rule: No prose-only closure. Every acceptance condition and evidence item must pass on the exact candidate; otherwise mark NOT_CLAIMED or NO_GO.
mandatory_counterfactuals: valid syntax with contradictory semantics
authenticated but unauthorized producer
stale yet correctly signed data
correct version string with wrong contract/algorithm digest
clean synthetic result under nonrepresentative distribution
timeout or capacity exhaustion followed by convenience fallback
feature unification activating a privileged/experimental path
partial migration accepted through default values
crash between decision/output and durable evidence
simple but odd input unlikely to resemble training examples
preconditions: HEAD equals 94e2f8cc01f352d2bf899b7f656997f143a2588f or an approved updated audit cut exists.
Every in-scope tracked/generated file is present in FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv and assigned to a named reviewer.
The current behavior, public claim tier, assumptions, affected consumers, and rollback are recorded before editing.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line, including tests, examples, generated output, workflow, documentation, and relevant Git history; record blob and SHA-256 identities.
Write a current-behavior table covering inputs, validation, state, transitions, outputs, errors, resources, time, identity, persistence, concurrency, and public claims.
Enumerate all plausible semantic interpretations and construct at least one counterexample or misuse for each rejected interpretation.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable IDs and exact accepted/rejected cases; obtain lead approval before implementation.
Implement the smallest coherent change and delete, quarantine, or version any contradictory legacy/default/fallback path.
Add positive, malformed, empty, min/max, non-finite, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future, capacity, cancellation, restart, and migration tests as applicable.
Add property/metamorphic, differential/oracle, fuzz, mutation-sensitive, concurrency, resource, and integration tests appropriate to the claim tier.
Generate evidence from a clean checkout with exact tools, commands, configs, seeds, data/models, platform/hardware, raw outputs, exit status, and checksums.
Update schemas, API snapshots, migration, examples, README/docs, claim/evidence ledger, support matrix, and exact downstream pins.
Have an independent reviewer reproduce the decisive evidence and review every changed line plus relevant unchanged context.
Run the subsystem gate, complete wave gate, full locked CI, and every affected downstream conformance job.
Close only after all twenty lenses are resolved with evidence; otherwise remove/narrow the claim and leave the task OPEN.
required_evidence: positive and negative vectors or fixtures
coverage, fuzz/mutation or model results where applicable

review_questions.L01: Can a reader infer more than the exact evidence tier supports? Separate implemented, verified, validated, deployment-qualified, field-validated, and not-claimed.
review_questions.L02: Are inputs, state, transitions, outputs, invariants, units, and failure states explicit rather than implied by names?
review_questions.L03: Are definitions, assumptions, estimands, finite-sample behavior, multiplicity, uncertainty, calibration, and failure regions correct and explicit?
review_questions.L04: Can invalid combinations be constructed through safe APIs, deserialization, FFI, feature flags, defaults, or migration?
review_questions.L05: Are clock domains, freshness, deadlines, sessions, epochs, generations, sequence numbers, duplicates, restart, and rollover unambiguous?
review_questions.L06: Can every result, command, model, dataset, scene, schema, configuration, build, and evidence artifact be traced immutably?
review_questions.L07: Are canonical bytes, domain separation, algorithms, identities, rotation, revocation, downgrade resistance, and failure semantics complete?
review_questions.L08: Who may observe, advise, intend, authorize, publish, apply, and confirm? Can any fallback or optional component widen authority?
review_questions.L09: What happens with duplicates, unknown fields, unsafe integers, NaN/Inf, Unicode ambiguity, malformed nesting, path tricks, and contradictory metadata?
review_questions.L10: Are bytes, allocations, dimensions, states, queues, retries, threads, log volume, GPU/CPU work, and serialization bounded before expensive work?
review_questions.L11: Are initialization, callback ownership, races, cancellation, selected events, cleanup, crash consistency, and shutdown fully specified?
review_questions.L12: Can independent clean machines reproduce code generation, builds, fixtures, simulations, proofs, benchmarks, evidence, and archives?
review_questions.L13: Is the stable surface minimal and are panic, ownership, allocation, thread safety, features, MSRV/runtime, and compatibility explicit?
review_questions.L14: Is there one normative semantic source and do all languages/encodings accept and reject the same corpus?
review_questions.L15: Can invalid profiles start? Are paths, secrets, permissions, ACLs, certificates, environments, startup order, and rollback safe?
review_questions.L16: Can disabled, unavailable, stale, incompatible, insufficient, anomalous, denied, and internal-fault states be distinguished without leaking secrets?
review_questions.L17: Does each claim have the right proof: theorem, model, property, fuzz, mutation, clean-room, real-router, statistical, or physical evidence?
review_questions.L18: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Prisoma, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, ROS, and external authority relationships explicit and acyclic?
review_questions.L19: Are naming, docs, examples, runbooks, review roles, incident response, support, deprecation, and withdrawal usable under stress?
review_questions.L20: How would a malicious peer, rushed operator, odd-but-simple dataset, future maintainer, or AI-generated patch falsify the intended claim?

source_requirements.completion_rule: No prose-only closure. Every acceptance condition and evidence item must pass on the exact candidate; otherwise mark NOT_CLAIMED or NO_GO.
mandatory_counterfactuals: valid syntax with contradictory semantics
authenticated but unauthorized producer
stale yet correctly signed data
correct version string with wrong contract/algorithm digest
clean synthetic result under nonrepresentative distribution
timeout or capacity exhaustion followed by convenience fallback
feature unification activating a privileged/experimental path
partial migration accepted through default values
crash between decision/output and durable evidence
simple but odd input unlikely to resemble training examples
preconditions: HEAD equals 94e2f8cc01f352d2bf899b7f656997f143a2588f or an approved updated audit cut exists.
Every in-scope tracked/generated file is present in FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv and assigned to a named reviewer.
The current behavior, public claim tier, assumptions, affected consumers, and rollback are recorded before editing.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line, including tests, examples, generated output, workflow, documentation, and relevant Git history; record blob and SHA-256 identities.
Write a current-behavior table covering inputs, validation, state, transitions, outputs, errors, resources, time, identity, persistence, concurrency, and public claims.
Enumerate all plausible semantic interpretations and construct at least one counterexample or misuse for each rejected interpretation.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable IDs and exact accepted/rejected cases; obtain lead approval before implementation.
Implement the smallest coherent change and delete, quarantine, or version any contradictory legacy/default/fallback path.
Add positive, malformed, empty, min/max, non-finite, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future, capacity, cancellation, restart, and migration tests as applicable.
Add property/metamorphic, differential/oracle, fuzz, mutation-sensitive, concurrency, resource, and integration tests appropriate to the claim tier.
Generate evidence from a clean checkout with exact tools, commands, configs, seeds, data/models, platform/hardware, raw outputs, exit status, and checksums.
Update schemas, API snapshots, migration, examples, README/docs, claim/evidence ledger, support matrix, and exact downstream pins.
Have an independent reviewer reproduce the decisive evidence and review every changed line plus relevant unchanged context.
Run the subsystem gate, complete wave gate, full locked CI, and every affected downstream conformance job.
Close only after all twenty lenses are resolved with evidence; otherwise remove/narrow the claim and leave the task OPEN.

coverage, fuzz/mutation or model results where applicable

mandatory_counterfactuals: valid syntax with contradictory semantics
authenticated but unauthorized producer
stale yet correctly signed data
correct version string with wrong contract/algorithm digest
clean synthetic result under nonrepresentative distribution
timeout or capacity exhaustion followed by convenience fallback
feature unification activating a privileged/experimental path
partial migration accepted through default values
crash between decision/output and durable evidence
simple but odd input unlikely to resemble training examples

procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line, including tests, examples, generated output, workflow, documentation, and relevant Git history; record blob and SHA-256 identities.
Write a current-behavior table covering inputs, validation, state, transitions, outputs, errors, resources, time, identity, persistence, concurrency, and public claims.
Enumerate all plausible semantic interpretations and construct at least one counterexample or misuse for each rejected interpretation.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable IDs and exact accepted/rejected cases; obtain lead approval before implementation.
Implement the smallest coherent change and delete, quarantine, or version any contradictory legacy/default/fallback path.
Add positive, malformed, empty, min/max, non-finite, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future, capacity, cancellation, restart, and migration tests as applicable.
Add property/metamorphic, differential/oracle, fuzz, mutation-sensitive, concurrency, resource, and integration tests appropriate to the claim tier.
Generate evidence from a clean checkout with exact tools, commands, configs, seeds, data/models, platform/hardware, raw outputs, exit status, and checksums.
Update schemas, API snapshots, migration, examples, README/docs, claim/evidence ledger, support matrix, and exact downstream pins.
Have an independent reviewer reproduce the decisive evidence and review every changed line plus relevant unchanged context.
Run the subsystem gate, complete wave gate, full locked CI, and every affected downstream conformance job.
Close only after all twenty lenses are resolved with evidence; otherwise remove/narrow the claim and leave the task OPEN.

coverage, fuzz/mutation or model results where applicable

review_questions.L01: Can a reader infer more than the exact evidence tier supports? Separate implemented, verified, validated, deployment-qualified, field-validated, and not-claimed.
review_questions.L02: Are inputs, state, transitions, outputs, invariants, units, and failure states explicit rather than implied by names?
review_questions.L03: Are definitions, assumptions, estimands, finite-sample behavior, multiplicity, uncertainty, calibration, and failure regions correct and explicit?
review_questions.L04: Can invalid combinations be constructed through safe APIs, deserialization, FFI, feature flags, defaults, or migration?
review_questions.L05: Are clock domains, freshness, deadlines, sessions, epochs, generations, sequence numbers, duplicates, restart, and rollover unambiguous?
review_questions.L06: Can every result, command, model, dataset, scene, schema, configuration, build, and evidence artifact be traced immutably?
review_questions.L07: Are canonical bytes, domain separation, algorithms, identities, rotation, revocation, downgrade resistance, and failure semantics complete?
review_questions.L08: Who may observe, advise, intend, authorize, publish, apply, and confirm? Can any fallback or optional component widen authority?
review_questions.L09: What happens with duplicates, unknown fields, unsafe integers, NaN/Inf, Unicode ambiguity, malformed nesting, path tricks, and contradictory metadata?
review_questions.L10: Are bytes, allocations, dimensions, states, queues, retries, threads, log volume, GPU/CPU work, and serialization bounded before expensive work?
review_questions.L11: Are initialization, callback ownership, races, cancellation, selected events, cleanup, crash consistency, and shutdown fully specified?
review_questions.L12: Can independent clean machines reproduce code generation, builds, fixtures, simulations, proofs, benchmarks, evidence, and archives?
review_questions.L13: Is the stable surface minimal and are panic, ownership, allocation, thread safety, features, MSRV/runtime, and compatibility explicit?
review_questions.L14: Is there one normative semantic source and do all languages/encodings accept and reject the same corpus?
review_questions.L15: Can invalid profiles start? Are paths, secrets, permissions, ACLs, certificates, environments, startup order, and rollback safe?
review_questions.L16: Can disabled, unavailable, stale, incompatible, insufficient, anomalous, denied, and internal-fault states be distinguished without leaking secrets?
review_questions.L17: Does each claim have the right proof: theorem, model, property, fuzz, mutation, clean-room, real-router, statistical, or physical evidence?
review_questions.L18: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Prisoma, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, ROS, and external authority relationships explicit and acyclic?
review_questions.L19: Are naming, docs, examples, runbooks, review roles, incident response, support, deprecation, and withdrawal usable under stress?
review_questions.L20: How would a malicious peer, rushed operator, odd-but-simple dataset, future maintainer, or AI-generated patch falsify the intended claim?
